intent: Generate graph-ready edge candidates from section vectors, claims, tags, and structural section roles without over-owning graph storage or render policy. Prefer real upstream packets; when claims or tags are missing for the current article, derive lightweight local signals and mark them as mocked.

input.format: Vectors provide reusable similarity objects. Section map provides stable ids + heading hierarchy. Tags provide chi/law overlap signals. Claims provide claim overlap and support-needed hooks. Math payload can flag equation-bearing sections for structural links.

output.format: graph-edges.json is the source of truth. CSV is workbook-friendly. graph-review.md is the human-readable QA pass for the lane.

edge_policy.calibration_expectation: Calibration must include checklist -> kill_condition and checklist -> risk section edges. These may arrive as STRUCTURAL_DEPENDENCY edges with explicit evidence notes.
edge_policy.idempotence: Running twice against the same inputs should produce the same sorted edge list.
edge_policy.storage_boundary: Lane 09 emits candidate edges only. It does not own graph persistence, Postgres writes, or site rendering.

loopback_conditions: 08 vectors missing or row count does not cover every section_id.
Edge generation produces zero edges for a multi-section article.
Edge explosion exceeds 5x section_count without a documented reason.
Claims are missing for a non-calibration article and no mocked local claim signals were recorded.

models: lightweight local signal extraction when claims/tags absent
